import React from 'react'; import UserDropdown from './UserDropdown'; import './UserDropdownExample.css'; /** * Example usage of the UserDropdown component * * This file demonstrates how to integrate the UserDropdown component * into your trading dashboard or any other page. */ const UserDropdownExample = () => { return (

Trading Dashboard Header

Modern User Dropdown Example

📊 TradingHub
Dashboard Market Portfolio Learn
{/* This is where you'd use the UserDropdown component */}

Component Features

  • ✨ Dark theme with glassmorphism effects
  • 🎯 Two organized sections (Settings & Help)
  • ⚡ Smooth animations and hover effects
  • 📱 Fully responsive design
  • ♿ Accessibility compliant (WCAG)
  • 🎨 Customizable colors and styling
  • 🔔 Active state management
  • 🚀 Performance optimized

Integration Guide

Step 1: Install react-icons (if not already installed)

npm install react-icons

Step 2: Import the component

{`import UserDropdown from './components/UserDropdown';`}

Step 3: Use in your navbar/header

{`export default function Header() {
  return (
    

      {/* Your other navbar items */}
      
    

  );
}`}

Step 4: Customize the menu items (optional)

Edit the settingsItems and supportItems arrays in UserDropdown.jsx

Customization Tips

Change Colors

Edit the color variables in UserDropdown.css. Primary color is #64c8ff

Add Item Actions

Modify the handleMenuItemClick function to handle different items

Connect to Navigation

Use React Router's useNavigate() in the click handler to navigate between pages

Add User Avatar

Replace the .user-avatar content with an img tag for a profile picture

); }; export default UserDropdownExample;

Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

  82 |

> 84 |     setLoading(true);
     |     ^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect

  87 |       .catch(() => setData(null))

import React from 'react'; import { Newspaper, Globe, ArrowUpRight, ArrowDownRight, TrendingUp, DollarSign, Activity } from 'lucide-react'; const PerformanceCard = ({ label, value, isUp }) => (
{label}
{isUp ? : } {value}
); export default function AnalysisBottom({ stock }) { if (!stock) return null; const news = [ { source: 'Reuters', time: '24m ago', title: 'Reliance Industries expands clean energy portfolio with new acquisition.' }, { source: 'Economic Times', time: '2h ago', title: 'Why institutional investors are bullish on RIL ahead of quarterly results.' }, { source: 'CNBC', time: '4h ago', title: 'Energy sector outperformance leads Nifty rally; RIL hits 52-week high.' } ]; return (
{}

Performance Benchmarks

VS NIFTY 50 (+1.2%)
{}

Company Overview

Reliance Industries Limited (RIL) is a Fortune 500 company and the largest private sector corporation in India. It has evolved from being a textiles and polyester company into an integrated player across energy, materials, retail, entertainment and digital services. RIL's motto "Growth is Life" aptly captures its ever-evolving spirit.

CEO Mukesh Ambani
Founded 1966
Headquarters Mumbai, India
Employess 2.6M +
{}

Fundamentals Snapshot

Revenue (TTM) ₹9.8T +15.2% YoY
Net Profit ₹780B +11.8% YoY
Growth Rate 14.6% Industry Avg: 12.1%
{}

Market Catalysts

{news.map((item, i) => (
{item.source} {item.time}

{item.title}

))}
); }

{}
Smart Insights

Breakout Confirmed: Price closing above ₹2,850 supply base with high volume.

Indicator Alignment: RSI (66) and MACD show strong supporting bullish momentum.

{}
Sector Strength
Energy Sector
{peers.map((peer, i) => (
{peer.name} {peer.momentum === 'high' && }
{peer.change} {peer.up ? : }
))}
{}
Similar Opportunities
{opportunities.map((opp, i) => (
{opp.name} {opp.pattern} Pattern
{opp.change}
))}
{}
Unusual Activity
14:22:15 Volume Spike: +400% avg
13:45:00 Gap Up Confirmation
); }

import React from 'react'; import { Target, ShieldAlert, BarChart3, TrendingUp, Info } from 'lucide-react'; export default function TradeInsights() { const riskReward = 3.2; return (

Trade Intelligence

Setup Active
Entry Zone
₹2,855 - 2,870

Wait for pull-back to 21 EMA

Profit Target
₹3,040 - 3,120

Projected 8.5% Upside

Stop Loss
₹2,810.00

Below previous swing low

Risk / Reward
1 : {riskReward}
{}
Why this Opportunity?

Trend Alignment: Stock is trading above all major MAs (20, 50, 200) on both Daily and Weekly charts.

Volume Confirmation: Recent 2% candle was backed by 1.5x average weekly volume.

Sector Tailwinds: Major peers in the Energy sector have shown 3.2% relative outperformance this week.

Catalyst: Technical breakout above long-term resistance of ₹2,850 now confirmed as Support.

); }

import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { BarChart2, Activity, Settings2, Grid, TrendingUp, TrendingDown } from 'lucide-react'; export default function MinimalTraderChart({ symbol, price, isPositive }) { const [timeframe, setTimeframe] = useState('1D'); const [chartType, setChartType] = useState('candlestick'); const [showIndicators, setShowIndicators] = useState(false); return (
{/* Top Bar: Timeframes and Controls */}
{['1m', '5m', '10m', '15m', '30m', '1H', '4H', '1D'].map(tf => ( ))}
{showIndicators && (
Active Indicators
{['EMA (20, 50)', 'RSI (14)', 'MACD (12, 26, 9)'].map(ind => ( ))}
)}
{/* The Chart Mock */}
Minimal Interactive Chart Region
{/* Price axis mock */}
{(price * 1.01).toFixed(2)} {(price * 1.005).toFixed(2)} {price.toFixed(2)} {(price * 0.995).toFixed(2)} {(price * 0.99).toFixed(2)}
{/* Time axis mock */}
10:00 11:00 12:00 13:00 14:00 15:00
{/* Compact Info Row */}
Current Trend
{isPositive ? : } {isPositive ? 'Bullish' : 'Bearish'}
RSI (14)
64.5 (Neutral)
Volume Status
Above Average
); }

Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

import React from 'react'; import { Link } from 'react-router-dom'; export default function PrivacyPolicy() { return (
RADAR
Log In

Privacy Policy

Last updated: May 2026

1. Information We Collect

At Radar Financial Analytics, we collect information to provide better services to our users. This includes:

  • Information you provide to us directly (such as when you create an account).
  • Information we collect automatically when you use our services (such as IP address, device type, and usage patterns).
  • Information from third-party services (such as OAuth providers if you choose to link them).

2. How We Use Your Information

We use the information we collect for various purposes, including:

  • Providing, maintaining, and improving our services.
  • Personalizing your experience on the Radar platform.
  • Communicating with you about updates, security alerts, and support messages.
  • Ensuring compliance with our Terms of Service and applicable laws.

3. Data Security

We implement robust security measures to protect your personal information from unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. However, no method of transmission over the internet or electronic storage is 100% secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

4. Your Rights

Depending on your location, you may have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. You can manage your account settings directly within the Radar platform or contact our support team for assistance.

5. Contact Us

If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at support@radar-analytics.com.

© 2026 Radar Financial Analytics. All rights reserved.

Privacy Policy Terms of Service
); }

import React from 'react'; import { Link } from 'react-router-dom'; export default function TermsOfService() { return (
RADAR
Log In

Terms of Service

Last updated: May 2026

1. Acceptance of Terms

By accessing or using the Radar Financial Analytics platform ("Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, you may not access or use the Service.

2. Description of Service

Radar provides real-time market research, analytics, and trading simulation tools. The information provided on the platform is for informational and educational purposes only and should not be construed as financial advice.

3. User Accounts

To access certain features of the Service, you must register for an account. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account.

4. Prohibited Conduct

You agree not to:

  • Use the Service for any illegal purpose or in violation of any local, state, national, or international law.
  • Attempt to gain unauthorized access to any portion of the Service or any other systems or networks connected to the Service.
  • Interfere with or disrupt the operation of the Service or the servers or networks used to make the Service available.
  • Scrape, data mine, or otherwise programmatically extract data from the Service without express written permission.

5. Disclaimer of Warranties

The Service is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind, either express or implied. Radar Financial Analytics does not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of the information provided through the Service.

6. Limitation of Liability

In no event shall Radar Financial Analytics be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising out of or relating to your use of the Service or the information provided therein.

7. Contact Information

If you have any questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us at legal@radar-analytics.com.

© 2026 Radar Financial Analytics. All rights reserved.

Privacy Policy Terms of Service
); }

Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

  400 |     if (!symbol) return;
  401 |     let active = true;
> 402 |     setLoading(true);
      |     ^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  403 |
  404 |     Promise.allSettled([
  405 |       fetchTechnicalSummary('stock', symbol),
